title: IR Schema Versioning
summary: Generated reader for C2 IR schema versioning: CanonicalEvent shape, scope-aware envelope validation, NativeConfigVersion dynamic checks, fake placeholder rejection, and reducer/version boundary anchors.
invariants: CanonicalEvent is append-only; shape changes introduce new schema versions instead of mutating existing events.
EventScope owns field presence: absent values are represented by lower scope, not placeholder strings.
ValidateEnvelope is the static gate; ValidateEnvelopeWithRunContext resolves phase-dependent NCV requirements.
TaskScope and RunScope transition events require FSMVersion.
SystemScope and RuntimeScope never carry FSMVersion.
RunScope requires runtime identity and capability surface fields before execution context checks.
Fake placeholder values are rejected before reducer semantics are considered.
TaskIR and snapshots are derived views; event log remains truth-of-record.

loop.observation: IR schema versioning had executable envelope validators and tests, but the reader was a long hand-maintained SSOT with rules that could drift from code.
loop.hypothesis: Generating the reader from validator probes, reflection, and explicit scope rule counts will make schema governance observable and prevent prose from overstating runtime enforcement.
loop.execute: Reflect CanonicalEvent and RunContext, probe scope validators, fake placeholders, FSMVersion, native-config context, render the reader, and upload evidence in CI.
loop.evaluate: The verifier fails on stale markdown, malformed manifest, workflow evidence gaps, scope rule drift, validation entrypoint drift, fake placeholder drift, or field-count drift.
loop.retrospective: IR schema versioning becomes an executable validator surface rather than a narrative description of intended enforcement.
